channel.title: CyberNetSec - Ransomware
channel.description: Latest ransomware threat intelligence - the last 10 articles

item.title: Q2 Ransomware Attacks Up 3%; VPNs Remain Top Initial Access Vector
item.description:
According to NCC Group's Q2 2026 threat report, global ransomware attacks increased by 3% to 2,229 incidents. The industrial sector was most targeted, and North America remained the most impacted region. For the fifth straight quarter, the Qilin ransomware group was the most active. The report criti...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Vulnerability, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: Alkegen Data Breach Exposes Social Security Numbers, Health Records
item.description:
Specialty materials manufacturer Alkegen (formerly ASP Unifrax Holdings) has disclosed a data breach that may have exposed the Social Security numbers and personal health records of affected individuals. The incident, first claimed by the Akira ransomware group on April 23, 2026, allegedly involved ...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Ransomware, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: Government agencies hit by ransomware daily in H1 2026, study finds
item.description:
A new study from Comparitech reveals a troubling trend: government organizations were successfully attacked by ransomware at an average rate of one per day in the first half of 2026. The 187 documented attacks mark a 13% increase from the latter half of 2025. The United States was the most frequent ...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Threat Intelligence, Data Breach

Read Full Article →

item.title: Ransomware Attacks in Europe Skyrocket by 55% as Supply Chains Become Prime Targets
item.description:
A new report from cyber risk management firm Black Kite reveals a staggering 55.1% year-over-year increase in ransomware attacks across Europe in the first four months of 2026. The manufacturing sector was the most heavily targeted industry. Five countries—Germany, the UK, France, Italy, and Spain—a...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Threat Intelligence, Supply Chain Attack

Read Full Article →

item.title: CKR Consulting Engineers Hit by 'payload' Ransomware Group
item.description:
The U.S.-based firm CKR Consulting Engineers has been listed as a victim by the 'payload' ransomware group. The data breach was discovered and reported on the group's leak site on July 19, 2026. This incident highlights the ongoing threat of ransomware to professional services firms, which often hol...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Data Breach, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: Governments Move Toward Banning Ransomware Payments Amid Rising Attacks
item.description:
As ransomware attacks become more frequent and sophisticated, governments globally are considering and implementing bans on ransom payments to cybercriminals. A July 20, 2026 report highlights a planned U.K. ban for public sector and critical infrastructure organizations as a key example of this gro...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Ransomware

Read Full Article →

item.title: Ransomware attack on Coca-Cola's Fairlife halts U.S. milk production
item.description:
Fairlife, a dairy subsidiary of The Coca-Cola Company, has suspended all U.S. production after a ransomware attack compromised its IT and operational technology networks. The breach was detected on July 16, 2026, leading to an immediate shutdown of affected systems and the launch of an incident resp...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Cyberattack, Industrial Control Systems

Read Full Article →

item.title: Identity Attacks Now Top Ransomware Vector, Eclipsing Exploits
item.description:
According to the Sophos 'State of Ransomware 2026' report, compromised identities are now the primary root cause of ransomware attacks, accounting for 79% of incidents. For the first time in four years, malicious email and phishing have surpassed software vulnerability exploitation as the top initia...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Threat Intelligence, Phishing

Read Full Article →

item.title: Kenyan President's Website Hacked, Attackers Demand Bitcoin Ransom
item.description:
The official website of Kenyan President William Ruto was compromised in a cyberattack on July 18, 2026. The government confirmed the incident and temporarily restricted access to conduct an investigation. While officials stated there is no evidence of sensitive data theft, reports indicate the atta...

Severity: High

Categories: Cyberattack, Threat Intelligence, Ransomware

Read Full Article →

item.title: New 'BL4CK SP1D3R' Ransomware Uses Double Extortion Tactics
item.description:
A new ransomware variant named 'BL4CK SP1D3R' has been identified targeting Windows systems. The malware employs double extortion tactics, first exfiltrating sensitive files and then encrypting them, appending a '.bl4ck' extension. A ransom note, 'BL4CK_SP1D3R_README.txt', threatens to leak the stol...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Malware, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →
